name: Cipollone v. Liggett, Inc.
summary: The Supreme Court held that the Federal Cigarette Labeling and Advertising Act did not preempt all state law damages claims based on failure to warn, because the statute did not expressly preempt such claims and Congress did not clearly intend to eliminate state remedies.
significance: Cipollone is foundational preemption jurisprudence establishing that courts should not infer preemption absent clear congressional intent, and that federal approval of a product does not necessarily preempt state law claims.
